```{r}
      name = "Turbine State"                     # Legend title for clarity
    ) +
    # 4) Labels and titles with clear, publication-quality text
    labs(
      title = "RMS Displacement Across Wind Speed Bins",
      x     = "Wind Speed (Centered & Scaled bins)",
      y     = "RMS (nm)"
    ) +
    # 5) Refined theme settings for presentation
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14), 
      axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),   # Tilt x-axis labels for readability
      legend.position  = "bottom",                              # Place legend below plot
      legend.title     = element_text(size = 12),               # Legend title size
      legend.text      = element_text(size = 10),               # Legend text size
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90"),        # Subtle grid lines
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()                        # Remove minor grid lines
    )



```
```{r}
###############################################################################
# Interquartile Range of RMS by Wind Speed Bins
# Purpose: Assess how the variability (IQR) of RMS displacement (nm) changes
#          across discrete bins of centered & scaled wind speed for Background 
#          vs Operational turbine states.
###############################################################################

# Assumes dplyr and ggplot2 are already loaded.

iqr_ws <- energy_df %>%
  mutate(
    # Convert integrated energy (nm²) to RMS displacement (nm)
    RMS    = sqrt(E),
    # Create descriptive factor for turbine state
    Type   = factor(
               Operational,
               levels = c(0, 1),
               labels = c("Background", "Operational")
             ),
    # Bin centered & scaled wind speed into 6 equal-width categories
    ws_bin = cut(
               ws_cs,
               breaks        = 6,
               include.lowest = TRUE
             )
  ) %>%
  group_by(Type, ws_bin) %>%
  summarise(
    # Compute interquartile range of RMS within each wind-speed bin and state
    IQR = IQR(RMS, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Plot IQR vs wind-speed bin for each turbine state
ggplot(iqr_ws, aes(x = ws_bin, y = IQR, color = Type, group = Type)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +                            # Connect IQR points across bins
  geom_point(size = 2) +                           # Highlight each bin’s IQR
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Background"  = "#E69F00",                   # Orange for turbine OFF
      "Operational" = "#0072B2"                    # Blue for turbine ON
    ),
    name = "Turbine State"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Interquartile Range of RMS by Wind Speed Bin",
    x     = "Wind Speed (Centered & Scaled bins)",
    y     = "IQR of RMS (nm)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )


```


```{r}
###############################################################################
# Visualising Model Complexity via Effective Degrees of Freedom
# Purpose: Extract and plot the EDF for each smooth term in the full GAM,
#          providing a clear summary of the relative complexity (wiggliness)
#          of each smooth component.
###############################################################################

# 1) Extract the smooth term table from the GAM summary
#    - summary(gam_energy)$s.table contains estimated degrees of freedom (edf)
#      and test statistics for each smooth term.
edf_df <- as.data.frame(summary(gam_energy)$s.table)

# 2) Add a 'term' column from the row names to identify each smooth
edf_df$term <- rownames(edf_df)

# 3) Plot EDF by smooth term ordered from most to least complex
#    - geom_col shows a bar for each term’s edf
#    - Bar fill "#56B4E9" provides a consistent, publication-quality palette
ggplot(edf_df, aes(x = reorder(term, -edf), y = edf)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#56B4E9") +
  labs(
    title = "Model Complexity by Smooth Term",
    x     = "Smooth Term",                      # Label indicates which smooth
    y     = "Effective Degrees of Freedom (EDF)"# EDF measures wiggliness (higher = more complex)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +               # Clean theme for clarity
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),   # Tilt x-axis labels for readability
    plot.title  = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5) # Center and bold the title
  )

```
```{r}
###############################################################################
# Density Distribution of RMS Displacement by Turbine State
# Purpose: Compare the full distribution of RMS noise for Background vs 
#          Operational periods, with the regulatory threshold clearly marked.
###############################################################################

# Load required libraries (skip if already loaded earlier)
library(dplyr)   # Data manipulation: mutate, %>%, etc.
library(ggplot2) # Plotting: ggplot, geom_density, etc.

# 1) Prepare the data frame: compute RMS and create descriptive factor 'Type'
density_df <- energy_df %>%
  mutate(
    RMS  = sqrt(E),                                       # Convert energy (nm²) to RMS (nm)
    Type = factor(
      Operational, 
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Background", "Operational")
    )                                                     # Human-readable turbine state
  )

# 2) Plot the density curves with regulatory limit annotation
ggplot(density_df, aes(x = RMS, fill = Type)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +                             # Semi-transparent density curves
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0.336,                                   # Regulatory RMS limit (nm)
    linetype    = "dashed", 
    color       = "red"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x     = 0.36,                                          # Place text just above the limit
    y     = 10,                                           # Adjust based on max density
    label = "Limit = 0.336 nm",
    color = "black",
    hjust = 0
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "Background"  = "#E69F00",                         # Gray for turbine OFF
      "Operational" = "#0072B2"                          # Red for turbine ON
    ),
    name = "Turbine State"                                # Legend title
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of RMS Displacement by Turbine State",
    x     = "RMS (nm)",                                   # X-axis label with units
    y     = "Density"                                     # Y-axis label
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +                         # Clean, legible theme
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "bottom",                           # Place legend below plot
    axis.title      = element_text(size = 11),
    axis.text       = element_text(size = 10)
  )

```

```{r}
# Diurnal Variation in Log Energy with Legend Placed Below

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

diurnal_df <- energy_df %>%
  group_by(HourOfDay, Operational) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_logE = mean(logE, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Status = factor(
      Operational,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Off", "On")
    )
  )

cols <- c(
  "Off" = "#E69F00",  
  "On"  = "#0072B2"   
)

ggplot(diurnal_df, aes(x = HourOfDay, y = mean_logE, color = Status)) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess",
    se     = FALSE,
    span   = 0.6,
    size   = 1.2
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, name = "Turbine Status") +
  labs(
    title = "Diurnal Variation in Log Energy",
    x     = "Hour of Day",
    y     = "log10(Energy)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "bottom",          # move legend below the plot
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90")
  )

```
```{r}
###############################################################################
# Day‐to‐Day Shifts from the s(Day) Term in gam_energy
# Purpose: Extract the estimated random‐effect intercepts for each date from 
#          the simplified GAM (gam_energy) and plot the daily log‐energy shifts 
#          with 95% confidence bands over time.
###############################################################################

# 0) Load necessary libraries for data manipulation, plotting, and axis formatting
library(dplyr)    # Data wrangling (mutate, transmute, etc.)
library(ggplot2)  # Plotting (ggplot, geom_ribbon, etc.)
library(scales)   # Date‐axis formatting helpers (date_labels, breaks)

# 1) Build a reference grid containing one row per date
#    - Other predictors fixed at typical values to isolate the day effect
ref_days <- tibble(
  Day         = factor(levels(energy_df$Day), levels = levels(energy_df$Day)),
  ws_cs       = 0,          # Centered & scaled wind‐speed at its mean
  WindDir     = 180,        # Wind direction fixed at 180° (southerly)
  HourOfDay   = 12,         # Hour of day fixed at noon UTC
  Operational = 0           # Background (turbine OFF) for baseline
)

# 2) Use predict(..., type="terms") to extract the s(Day) contributions
pred_terms <- predict(
  gam_energy,
  newdata = ref_days,
  type    = "terms",
  se.fit  = TRUE
)
# Identify which column corresponds to the s(Day) smooth
day_col <- grep("^s\\(Day\\)", colnames(pred_terms$fit))

# 3) Assemble a tidy data frame of estimates and 95% confidence intervals
re_df <- ref_days %>%
  transmute(
    Date     = as.Date(as.character(Day)),               # Convert factor to Date class
    Estimate = pred_terms$fit[, day_col],                # Estimated daily intercept shift
    Lower    = Estimate - 2 * pred_terms$se.fit[, day_col],  # Lower 95% CI
    Upper    = Estimate + 2 * pred_terms$se.fit[, day_col]   # Upper 95% CI
  )

# 4) Plot the day‐to‐day shifts with ribbon and line
p_day <- ggplot(re_df, aes(x = Date)) +
  # Confidence band for the daily effect
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = Lower, ymax = Upper, fill = "95% CI"),
    alpha = 0.3
  ) +
  # Line and points for the estimated shift
  geom_line(aes(y = Estimate, color = "s(Day)"), size = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(y = Estimate, color = "s(Day)"), size = 2) +
  
  # Manual scales to label legend entries clearly
  scale_color_manual(
    name   = "Component",
    values = c("s(Day)" = "lightseagreen")
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name   = "",
    values = c("95% CI" = "lightseagreen")
  ) +
  
  # Expand x‐axis to show every 3 months with YYYY‐MM labels
  scale_x_date(
    breaks      = seq(min(re_df$Date), max(re_df$Date), by = "3 months"),
    date_labels = "%Y-%m",
    expand      = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.15))  # Add 15 days margin at ends
  ) +
  
  # Titles and axis labels
  labs(
    title = "Day-to-Day Shifts (s(Day))",
    x     = "Date",
    y     = "Log₁₀-Energy Shift"
  ) +
  
  # Clean minimal theme and styling tweaks
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

# 5) Render the plot
print(p_day)

```
```{r}
###############################################################################
# Empirical CDF of RMS Displacement by Turbine State
# Purpose: Compare the full distributions of RMS seismic displacement (nm)
#          between Background and Operational periods using empirical
#          cumulative distribution functions (ECDFs).
###############################################################################

# Load required libraries for data manipulation and plotting
library(dplyr)    # For mutate(), %>%, and data frame operations
library(ggplot2)  # For ggplot(), stat_ecdf(), and theme_minimal()

# 1) Prepare the data frame for plotting
ecdf_df <- energy_df %>%
  mutate(
    # Convert integrated energy (nm²) to RMS displacement (nm)
    RMS   = sqrt(E),
    # Create a descriptive factor for turbine state (Background vs Operational)
    State = factor(
      Operational,
      levels = c(0, 1),                       # Ensure Background comes first
      labels = c("Background", "Operational") # Human-readable labels
    )
  )

# 2) Define custom colors for the two turbine states
cols <- c(
  "Background"  = "#E69F00",  
  "Operational" = "#0072B2"   
)

# 3) Plot the empirical CDFs
#    - stat_ecdf() computes the ECDF for each group and plots it
#    - Lines are colored by State for direct comparison
ggplot(ecdf_df, aes(x = RMS, color = State)) +
  stat_ecdf(size = 1.1) +                            # Thicker lines improve visibility
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, name = "State") +# Apply custom palette and legend title
  labs(
    title = "Empirical CDF of RMS Displacement",     # Plot title
    x     = "RMS (nm)",                              # X-axis label with units
    y     = "Empirical CDF"                          # Y-axis label
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +                    # Clean, minimal theme
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "bottom"                       # Place legend below plot
  )



```

```{r}
###############################################################################
# Predicted log₁₀(E) vs Wind Direction from the Full GAM
# Purpose: Generate model‐predicted log₁₀‐energy across the full 0°–360° range 
#          of wind directions for both Background and Operational states, 
#          holding other covariates at typical values to isolate the directional effect.
###############################################################################

# 0) Load required libraries
library(dplyr)   # Data manipulation: expand.grid, mutate, recode_factor
library(ggplot2) # Plotting: ggplot, geom_line, scale_x_continuous, theme_minimal

# 1) Build a prediction grid over WindDir and turbine state
#    - WindDir: 200 equally spaced points from 0° to 360°
#    - ws_cs    fixed at 0 (mean-centered wind speed)
#    - HourOfDay fixed at 12 (noon UTC) to remove diurnal variation
#    - Day      fixed at the first level to isolate only the s(Day) intercept
#    - Operational: factor levels 0 (Background) and 1 (Operational)
dir_grid <- expand.grid(
  WindDir     = seq(0, 360, length = 200),
  ws_cs       = 0,
  HourOfDay   = 12,
  Day         = factor(
                  levels(energy_df$Day)[1],
                  levels = levels(energy_df$Day)
                ),
  Operational = factor(c(0, 1), levels = c(0, 1))
)

# 2) Predict log₁₀‐energy for each grid point using the fitted GAM
#    - type = "response" returns fitted log₁₀(E) on the original scale
dir_grid$pred_logE <- predict(
  gam_energy,
  newdata = dir_grid,
  type    = "response"
)

# 3) Create a descriptive 'State' factor for plotting
dir_grid <- dir_grid %>%
  mutate(
    State = recode_factor(
      as.character(Operational),
      `0` = "Background",
      `1` = "Operational"
    )
  )

# 4) Plot the predictions
#    - geom_line() draws smooth curves of predicted log₁₀(E) vs WindDir
#    - scale_colour_manual() applies distinct colours for each state
#    - scale_x_continuous() labels the x‐axis in degrees at 45° increments
ggplot(dir_grid, aes(x = WindDir, y = pred_logE, colour = State)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    name   = "Turbine State",
    values = c(
      "Background"  = "#E69F00",  
      "Operational" = "#0072B2"   
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 360),
    breaks = seq(0, 360, 45),
    labels = paste0(seq(0, 360, 45), "°")
  ) +
  labs(
    title  = "Predicted log10(E) vs Wind Direction",
    x      = "Wind Direction (°)",
    y      = "Predicted log10(E)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )
```
